name: Sutlepa kabel
description: One of the oldest wooden buildings in Estonia. A wooden chapel from the Estonian Swedish (rannarootslased) settlement area, reconsecrated in 1989 and still used for sermons on major holy days.

name: Kolu korts
description: A historic roadside inn on the Tallinn-Tartu road. Now serves traditional Estonian meals to museum visitors, offering a taste of 19th-century travel culture.

name: Sassi-Jaani talu
description: A Western Estonian farm with a threshing house and storehouse, transferred to the museum in 1959-1960. The threshing house and storehouse burned in 1984 and were reopened in 1993.

subBuildings.name: Cattle-shed
subBuildings.originalLocation: Althärma farm, Jõeääre village, Märjamaa parish

subBuildings.name: Summer kitchen (conical tent)
subBuildings.originalLocation: Kõrvetaga farm, Määra village, Risti parish

name: Kostriaseme talu
description: An average-sized rental farm of about 30 hectares that paid rent to Hageri church manor. Represents the life of tenant farmers in Northern Estonia.

subBuildings.name: Two-room storehouse
subBuildings.originalLocation: Mõisaääre-Jaagu farm, Kelu village, Kirbla parish

subBuildings.name: Textile storehouse
subBuildings.originalLocation: Juhani farm, Aude village, Nissi parish

subBuildings.name: Cattle-shed
subBuildings.originalLocation: Mäe farm, Luiste village, Kullamaa parish

subBuildings.name: Summer kitchen
subBuildings.originalLocation: Keedelaugu farm, Ahtama village, Risti parish

name: Nuki talu
description: A smokehouse-type cotter dwelling from Western Estonia, representing the living conditions of landless rural workers in the late 19th century.

name: Harjapea talu (Kutsari-Harjapea)
description: An average-sized Viru County farm with an Empire-era dwelling, purchased for 4,000 silver roubles. The interior depicts life in the late 1930s, with outbuildings from Kutsari farm.

subBuildings.name: Storehouse
subBuildings.originalLocation: Uhe farm, Vihula village, Haljala parish

subBuildings.name: Cattle-shed
subBuildings.originalLocation: Orapere farm, Kõrbse village, Kadrina parish

subBuildings.name: Smoke sauna
subBuildings.originalLocation: Punga farm, Koila village, Viru-Nigula parish

name: Pulga talu
description: A rental farm characteristic of Northern Estonia, featuring limestone and stone construction including a threshing floor, smithy, and summer kitchen.

subBuildings.name: Storehouse with intermediate room
subBuildings.originalLocation: Sepa farm, Pudisoo village, Kuusalu parish

subBuildings.name: Two-storey storehouse
subBuildings.originalLocation: Jaani farm, Suurekõrve village, Kuusalu parish

subBuildings.name: Sauna combined with summer kitchen
subBuildings.originalLocation: Lauri farm, Kahala village, Kuusalu parish

subBuildings.name: Smithy
subBuildings.originalLocation: Oluva farm, Kahala village, Kuusalu parish

subBuildings.name: Hay shed (2)
subBuildings.originalLocation: Kangru farm, Kursi village, Kuusalu parish

name: Aarte talu
originalLocation: Virve village, Juminda peninsula, Kuusalu parish
description: A small fisherman's farm from the Juminda peninsula on the northern coast of Estonia, representing the life of coastal fishing communities.

subBuildings.name: Vergi Net Shed
subBuildings.originalLocation: Haljala parish

name: Sepa talu
originalLocation: Palometsa village, Rouge parish, Kasaritsa commune
description: A blacksmith's household with about 10 hectares of poor soil, where smithcraft was the main income. Belonged to Peep Harak, a former blacksmith of Sanna manor.

name: Ritsu talu
description: A cotter's household representing the poorest segment of rural Estonian society. The barn-dwelling was built in the 1860s and transferred to the museum in 1966.

name: Rusi talu
description: A farm of about 36 hectares, birthplace of sculptor Juhan Raudsepp (1896-1984). Buildings brought from Ala-Rusi farm and opened to visitors in 2002.

subBuildings.name: Storehouse (Ait-tall)
subBuildings.originalLocation: Pisu farm, Säre village, Urvaste parish

name: Setu talu (Vanatalo)
originalLocation: Lokova village, Pankjavitsa (now Pskov Oblast, Russia)
description: A fortified Setu farm with a distinctive three-row yard layout typical of eastern Setomaa. The dwelling and storehouse were brought to the museum in 1979 from territory now in Russia.

name: Natsi tuulik
originalLocation: Vanasauna farm, Natsi village, Mihkli parish
description: A large post windmill originally from Rabavere village, later sold to Natsi village. Enlarged by cotter Ants Kummel after WWI to grind flour for surrounding villages.

name: Kahala vesiveski
originalLocation: Moldri farm, Kahala village, Kuusalu parish
description: A small watermill built on Liiva spring, one of four watermills belonging to Kolga manor. Used to grind flour, rye coarse meal, gruel, and groats.

name: Kalma tuulik
description: A Dutch-type windmill with a cap-like rotating roof, built by Mustvee mill builder Jakob Sober for owner Juhan Sark. Brought to the museum in 1972 and rebuilt 1991-1995.

name: Kuie koolimaja
description: A rural parish school building relocated with help from the founders of Rocca al Mare school. Now serves as the museum's educational center, opened on September 1, 2000.

name: Orgmetsa tuletornipritsunemaja
description: A medium-sized volunteer firefighters' station from the Aravete-Albu firefighters' society (founded 1921). Contains manual fire engines, carts, barrels, hoses, and a bell tower.

name: Lau kylapood
description: A village shop built by distiller Jaan Meiberg from Ingliste manor. The exhibition depicts the shop as it would have looked in 1938, a prosperous era of Estonian economy.

name: Roosta talu
description: A Western Saaremaa farm representing the barter economy system, with about 45 hectares of land including 5 hectares of stony fields. Buildings from Kihelkonna and Mustjala parishes.

subBuildings.name: Cattle-shed
subBuildings.originalLocation: Mardi farm, Kõõru village, Mustjala parish, Saaremaa

subBuildings.name: Summer kitchen / Smithy
subBuildings.originalLocation: Annuse farm, Karala village, Kihelkonna parish, Saaremaa

subBuildings.name: Sauna
subBuildings.originalLocation: Matsi farm, Kugalepa village, Mustjala parish, Saaremaa

name: Juri-Jaagu talu
description: A 20th-century tenant farm from Muhu Island with 34 hectares of land. The family earned money from seasonal work due to poor stony soil and low pastureland yields.

subBuildings.name: Storehouse (replica)
subBuildings.originalLocation: Salu-Andrus farm, Rootsivere village, Muhu parish

subBuildings.name: Summer kitchen
subBuildings.originalLocation: Marjavälja farm, Külasema village, Muhu parish

subBuildings.name: Summer kitchen / Sauna / Cellar
subBuildings.originalLocation: Pärdi farm, Mäla village, Muhu parish

subBuildings.name: Cattle-shed
subBuildings.originalLocation: Mihkli farm, Lepiku village, Muhu parish

name: Kolga talu
description: A Hiiumaa tenant farm modeled on Pendi farm in Lelu village. Represents the spacious farmyard layout typical of Hiiumaa island farms.

subBuildings.name: Storehouse
subBuildings.originalLocation: Aadu farm, Lepiku village, Emmaste parish, Hiiumaa

subBuildings.name: Storehouse / Cattle shed (L-shaped)
subBuildings.originalLocation: Läku farm, Haldi village, Emmaste parish, Hiiumaa

subBuildings.name: Cellar
subBuildings.originalLocation: Mihkli farm, Tilga village, Emmaste parish, Hiiumaa

subBuildings.name: Smithy
subBuildings.originalLocation: Veoma farm, Vahtrepa village, Pühalepa parish, Hiiumaa

name: Vennastekoguduse palvemaja
originalLocation: Paka farm, Tuiu village, Mustjala parish
description: A Moravian Brethren prayer house, unique for having a black kitchen (uncommon in Estonia). The Moravian movement arrived in Estonia around 1730.

name: Hiiumaa randpuusekalaehoone
description: A temporary shelter built by Audru manor for Hiiumaa fishermen during the Baltic herring season. Was set on fire by an arsonist during the 1991 August putsch and later restored.

name: Peipsimaa vene maja (Petshonkini maja)
description: A dwelling from the Russian Old Believer settlement on the western shore of Lake Peipus. These communities were founded by Old Believers who fled Russia after the 18th-century Orthodox Church schism.

name: Kolhoosi korterelamu
originalLocation: Rabi village, Valga district (Järvesalu collective farm)
description: A four-unit Soviet-era silicate brick apartment house built for dairy barn workers. Transported 200 km to the museum in 2019, it depicts rural life from the 1960s through the 2010s.

name: Jaagu talu
description: A soldier's homestead apportioned from a state manor, with only 9 hectares of land including 2 hectares of fields. Opened at the museum in 1976.

subBuildings.name: Storehouse (replica)
subBuildings.originalLocation: Salu-Andrus farm, Rootsivere village, Muhu parish

subBuildings.name: Summer kitchen
subBuildings.originalLocation: Marjavälja farm, Külasema village, Muhu parish

subBuildings.name: Summer kitchen / Sauna / Cellar
subBuildings.originalLocation: Pärdi farm, Mäla village, Muhu parish

subBuildings.name: Cattle-shed
subBuildings.originalLocation: Mihkli farm, Lepiku village, Muhu parish
